segments.text: WOMAN: I've been reading your personal statement, Paul.
segments.translation: 女：保罗，我看过你的个人陈述了。

segments.text: First, let's talk about your work experience in South America.
segments.translation: 我们先来谈一谈你在南美洲的工作经历吧。

segments.text: What took you there? Was it to gain more fluency in Spanish?
segments.translation: 你当初为什么会去那儿呢？是为了练西班牙语吗？

segments.text: PAUL: Well, as I'm combining Spanish with Latin American studies, my main idea was to find out more about the way people lived there.
segments.translation: 保罗：这个，我的确是把西班牙语学习看做是拉丁美洲研究的一部分，但我去那儿的主要目的是了解当地人的生活方式。

segments.text: My spoken Spanish was already pretty good in fact.
segments.translation: 实际上我的西班牙语水平当时已经很好了。

segments.text: WOMAN: So you weren't too worried about language barriers?
segments.translation: 女：这么说来你当时是不太担心语言障碍了。

segments.text: PAUL: No. In fact, I ended up teaching English there, although that wasn't my original choice of work.
segments.translation: 保罗：不是的。实际上我最终在那边教英语，虽然这并不是我最开始想选的工作。

segments.text: WOMAN: I see. How did you find out about all this?
segments.translation: 女：了解。那你是怎么找到这个工作的呢？

segments.text: PAUL: I found an agency that runs all kinds of voluntary projects in South America.
segments.translation: 保罗：我找到一家运作各种南美洲的志愿者工作的组织。

segments.text: WOMAN: What kind of work?
segments.translation: 女：什么样的工作呢？

segments.text: PAUL: Well, there were several possibilities.
segments.translation: 保罗：有不同类型的工作。

segments.text: WOMAN: You mean construction? Engineering work?
segments.translation: 女：你是说跟建筑相关的工作吗？或者和机械相关的？

segments.text: PAUL: Yes, getting involved in building projects was an option.
segments.translation: 保罗：是的，参与建筑项目是一种选择。

segments.text: Then there was tourism - taking tourists for walks around the volcanoes - which I actually chose to do, and then there was work with local farmers.
segments.translation: 还有旅游相关的，就是带着游客逛火山，这也是我选择的工作。然后还有在当地农场的工作。

segments.text: WOMAN: But you didn't continue with that project. Why not?
segments.translation: 女：但你最终没有继续跟进这个项目。为什么呢？

segments.text: PAUL: Because I never really knew whether I'd be needed or not.
segments.translation: 保罗：因为我不知道这个工作是否需要我。

segments.text: I'd thought it might be difficult physically, but I was certainly fit enough ... no, I wanted to do something that had more of a proper structure to it, I suppose.
segments.translation: 我原以为这个工作对体能是个挑战，但我发现我完全能够胜任……我希望我能做一些更有计划性的工作。

segments.text: I get de-motivated otherwise.
segments.translation: 否则我会没有动力的。

segments.text: WOMAN: What do you think you learned from your experience?
segments.translation: 女：你觉得你从这些经历中学到了什么呢？

segments.text: It must have been a great opportunity to examine community life.
segments.translation: 这肯定是研究当地社区生活的绝佳机会。

segments.text: PAUL: Yes, but it was difficult at first to be accepted by the locals.
segments.translation: 保罗：没错，但一开始的时候自己还是很难被当地人接受。

segments.text: It was a very remote village and some of them were reluctant to speak to me - although they were always interested in my clothes and how much I'd had to pay for them.
segments.translation: 那是一个很偏僻的村子，然后村里很多人不愿意跟我说话，尽管他们一直对我的衣着以及它们的价格很感兴趣。

segments.text: WOMAN: Well, that's understandable.
segments.translation: 女：嗯，这可以理解。

segments.text: PAUL: Yes, but things soon improved.
segments.translation: 保罗：但情况很快就有所好转。

segments.text: What struck me was that when people became more comfortable with me and less suspicious, we really connected with each other in a meaningful way.
segments.translation: 人们开始不再那么疑虑，开始更愿意与我相处。我们之间建立起了有意义的联系。

segments.text: WOMAN: You made good friends?
segments.translation: 女：你有交到很好的朋友吗？

segments.text: PAUL: Yes, with two of the families in particular.
segments.translation: 保罗：是的，尤其是两个家庭。

segments.text: WOMAN: Good. What about management.
segments.translation: 女：很好。你那个项目的管理情况怎样？

segments.text: Did you have a project manager?
segments.translation: 你们当时有项目经理吗？

segments.text: PAUL: Yes and he gave me lots of advice and guidance.
segments.translation: 保罗：有的，他给了我很多建议和指导。

segments.text: WOMAN: And was he good at managing too?
segments.translation: 女：他的管理能力怎么样？

segments.text: PAUL: That wasn't his strong point!
segments.translation: 保罗：这倒不是他的强项。

segments.text: I think he was often more interested in the academic side of things than filing reports.
segments.translation: 我觉得他更愿意做学术研究，而不是弄文件报告。

segments.text: He was a bit of a dreamer.
segments.translation: 他有点理想主义。

segments.text: WOMAN: And did you have a contract?
segments.translation: 女：你当时签了合同吗？

segments.text: PAUL: I had to stay for a minimum of three months.
segments.translation: 保罗：我必须要待到至少三个月。

segments.text: My parents were surprised when I asked to stay longer - six months in the end.
segments.translation: 我后来申请了待更长时间，最后待了6个月，我父母都吃惊了。

segments.text: I was so happy there.
segments.translation: 我在那儿很开心。

segments.text: WOMAN: And did anything on the administration side of things surprise you?
segments.translation: 女：在管理方面有什么事情是出乎你意料的吗？

segments.text: What was the food and lodging like?
segments.translation: 比如饮食和住宿怎么样？

segments.text: PAUL: Simple ... but there was plenty to eat and I only paid seven dollars a day for that which was amazing really.
segments.translation: 保罗：吃的比较简单，不过量很大，而且一天只花费7美元，真的很棒。

segments.text: And they gave me all the equipment I needed ... even a laptop.
segments.translation: 他们给我提供了各种我需要的装备，甚至包括笔记本电脑。

segments.text: WOMAN: You didn't expect that then?
segments.translation: 女：这你当时没有想到吗？

segments.text: PAUL: No.
segments.translation: 保罗：没有。

segments.text: WOMAN: Well, I'll look forward to hearing more.
segments.translation: 女：好的，我想再了解一些别的。

segments.text: WOMAN: But now let's look at these modules.
segments.translation: 女：我们先来看一些课程模块。

segments.text: You'll need to start thinking about which ones you'll definitely want to study.
segments.translation: 你需要确定哪些是你明确想学的。

segments.text: The first one here is Gender Studies in Latin America.
segments.translation: 第一个是关于南美洲性别问题的研究。

segments.text: PAUL: Mmm ...
segments.translation: 保罗：嗯……

segments.text: WOMAN: It looks at how gender analysis is reconfiguring civil society in Latin America.
segments.translation: 女：它主要是研究性别分析是如何重建南美洲社会结构的。

segments.text: Women are increasingly occupying positions in government and in other elected leadership positions in Latin America.
segments.translation: 在南美洲，女人逐渐在政府或其他领导岗位任职。

segments.text: I think you'd find it interesting.
segments.translation: 我觉得你会喜欢的。

segments.text: PAUL: If it was to do with people in the villages rather than those in the public sphere, I would.
segments.translation: 保罗：如果是关于普通村民而不是公众人物的话，我想我可能会感兴趣。

segments.text: WOMAN: Okay. What about Second Language Acquisition?
segments.translation: 女：好吧。那关于第二外语习得的怎么样？

segments.text: PAUL: Do you think I'd find that useful?
segments.translation: 保罗：你觉得会对我有帮助吗？

segments.text: WOMAN: Well, you've had some practical experience in the field, I think it would be.
segments.translation: 女：你在这方面有一些实际经验，我觉得会。

segments.text: PAUL: I hadn't thought about that.
segments.translation: 保罗：我还没想过这些。

segments.text: I'll put that down as a definite, then.
segments.translation: 那就记下它吧。

segments.text: WOMAN: Okay. What about Indigenous Women's Lives. That sounds appropriate.
segments.translation: 女：好的。关于原住民女性生活的呢？听起来不错。

segments.text: PAUL: I thought so too, but I looked at last year's exam questions and that changed my mind.
segments.translation: 保罗：我也觉得， 但我看了去年的考试题目，所以改变主意了。

segments.text: WOMAN: Don't judge the value of the course on that.
segments.translation: 女：不要凭借考试题目来判断课程的价值。

segments.text: Maybe, talk to some other students first and we can talk about it again later.
segments.translation: 或许你可以先跟其他学生探讨一下，然后再选。

segments.text: PAUL: Okay.
segments.translation: 保罗：好的。

segments.text: WOMAN: Yes. And lastly, will you sign up for Portuguese lessons?
segments.translation: 女：恩，最后，你会报葡萄牙语课吗？

segments.text: PAUL: My Spanish is good, so would I find that module easy?
segments.translation: 保罗：我西班牙语挺好的，这样学葡萄牙语会轻松些吗？

segments.text: WOMAN: Not necessarily. Some people find that Spanish interferes with learning Portuguese ... getting the accent right too.
segments.translation: 女：不一定。有些人觉得西班牙语会干扰葡萄牙语的学习，以及语音的学习。

segments.text: It's quite different in a lot of ways.
segments.translation: 这两门语言有很多不同。

segments.text: PAUL: Well, I'd much sooner do something else, then.
segments.translation: 保罗：额，那我还是学点别的吧。

segments.text: WOMAN: Alright. Now, what we need to do is ...
segments.translation: 女：好的。现在我们要……
